title: graphRAG query safety policy (config.query_policy)
description: Frozen contract (Track 0 P5, DESIGN §21/§27.6, DR-002) for the query_policy block of a project's config. Human-authored, so unknown keys are rejected everywhere (additionalProperties: false) and every field is required: a typo'd or omitted limit silently falling back to a consumer-chosen default would fork the guardrail between query/MCP/Console consumers. Violations at query time are rejected with the typed GUARDRAIL_BLOCKED warning (§21), never executed. Evolution is additive; a breaking change bumps schema_version and adds a DESIGN §26 entry.

max_top_k.description: Upper bound on QueryRequest.top_k (§15). A cap of 0 would silently blank every retrieval — nonsense config, rejected.

max_graph_hops.description: Hop ceiling for graph traversals — the single source for Cypher hop caps too (§21 text_to_cypher hop 上限 references this; it is deliberately not duplicated inside text_to_cypher, so the two can never disagree).

max_sql_rows.description: Row ceiling for the sql mode's result sets (§21).

max_latency_ms.description: Query deadline; on timeout partial results + QUERY_TIMEOUT/PARTIAL_RESULTS semantics apply (§22), never a 500.

require_sources.description: Frozen true: both response contracts structurally mandate non-empty source_refs on every result (§16/§27.2, openapi.yaml + mcp_response.schema.json minItems 1), so a false here would promise payloads the frozen contracts reject. The field stays explicit rather than implied so the policy block reads complete against §21.

expose_debug.description: Whether the typed debug block (routing trace etc.) may be emitted to allowed callers (§16/§21); false → debug is null.

QueryMode.description: Kept identical to QueryMode in contracts/openapi.yaml, mcp_response.schema.json and golden.schema.json.

TextToSql.description: NL→SQL guardrail (§21/§27.6): dedicated read-only DB role, sqlglot AST validation before execution (never string matching alone), single statement, table whitelist, DDL/DML rejected; parse failure = reject.

enabled.description: Master switch for the sql mode. Disabled → sql queries are rejected/skipped with a typed warning (MODE_SKIPPED/GUARDRAIL_BLOCKED).

readonly.description: Frozen true (§21: 專用 DB 唯讀角色; §27.6: 唯讀角色) — a writable NL→SQL path violates the frozen guardrail, so it is unrepresentable. Explicit rather than implied so the block reads complete against §21.

allowed_tables.description: Table whitelist — queries touching anything else are rejected. May only be empty while enabled is false (see the enclosing if/then): an enabled sql mode with an empty whitelist is a config contradiction, not a deny-all switch (use enabled: false for that).

blocked_keywords.description: Defense-in-depth keyword list on top of the AST validation (lowercase canonical; matching is case-insensitive). §21 freezes the six-item minimum [insert, update, delete, drop, alter, truncate] — projects may extend the list, never shrink it below the frozen core (the contains clauses enforce this).

TextToCypher.description: NL→Cypher guardrail (§21/§27.6). Strategy: MCP graph tools default to parameterized query templates (neighbors/path/subgraph) — free NL→Cypher is an optional add-on (§27.6 選配), disabled unless enabled here, and even then restricted by a Cypher parser to the allowed clauses below. Procedure calls (CALL, and thus all of APOC) are blocked. Hop caps come from the top-level max_graph_hops.

enabled.description: Whether free NL→Cypher is available at all (§27.6: optional; parameterized templates remain the default graph path either way).

readonly.description: Frozen true (§21) — write clauses are additionally in the blocked list; a writable NL→Cypher path is unrepresentable.

allowed_clauses.description: Clause whitelist. §21 freezes the universe to MATCH/WHERE/RETURN/LIMIT — a project may restrict further but can never whitelist anything outside the frozen four (the items enum enforces this), so CALL/APOC can never be allowed in.

blocked.description: Blocklist on top of the whitelist (defense in depth; uppercase canonical — Cypher keywords are conventionally uppercase, matching is case-insensitive). §21 freezes the six-item minimum [CREATE, MERGE, DELETE, SET, REMOVE, CALL]; CALL covers every procedure including APOC. Extend, never shrink.

max_rows.description: Row ceiling for Cypher results (§21 row 上限; named to mirror text_to_sql.max_rows).
